lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The invoice number 'L101' contains the letter 'L' but this is not a LC reference — it is a standard invoice number. No field labelled 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit' or similar exists in the certificate.

Consignee 'Meelunie America Inc.' is identified in the invoice 'Recipient adress' field. Pairing validation passes with 2 of 3 criteria met.

checkpoint_results.certificate_value: Lantmännen Reppe AB, Jungmansgatan 8, SE-531 40, LIDKÖPING, Sweden
checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Lantmännen Reppe AB, Jungmansgatan 8, 53140 LIDKÖPING (issuer block) / SWEDISH VAT NUMBER: SE556000153801

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name 'Lantmännen Reppe AB' anywhere in the invoice.
comparison_steps.details: The name 'Lantmännen Reppe AB' is found explicitly in the invoice footer/issuer block at the bottom of the invoice page, with address 'Jungmansgatan 8, 53140 LIDKÖPING'. It is also found in the goods description text: 'Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB'. This constitutes an exact match per rule 4.1.0.

comparison_steps.description: Verify country Sweden for consignor via invoice.
comparison_steps.details: The VAT number SE556000153801 has prefix 'SE' confirming Sweden. The IBAN SE89... confirms Sweden. The text 'Sweden' appears in the Payment Details section. Country verified via multiple indirect identifiers per rule 4.1.2.1.

rules_applied.description: Corporate structure – consignor name found explicitly in invoice issuer block and goods description
Country verification via indirect identifiers – VAT prefix SE and IBAN prefix SE confirm Sweden

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Lantmännen Reppe AB, Jungmansgatan 8, SE-531 40, LIDKÖPING, Sweden'. The invoice is issued by 'Meelunie B.V.' in the TO/Name header, but the invoice issuer block at the bottom clearly shows 'Lantmännen Reppe AB, Jungmansgatan 8, 53140 LIDKÖPING' with Swedish VAT number SE556000153801. The consignor name is found explicitly in the invoice per rule 4.1.0 (corporate structure). The 'TO' field identifies the buyer (Meelunie B.V.), while the actual invoice issuer is Lantmännen Reppe AB. Country Sweden is confirmed via VAT prefix SE and IBAN prefix SE per rule 4.1.2.1. Address matches: Jungmansgatan 8, Lidköping. The only difference is the postal code format (SE-531 40 vs 53140) which is a minor formatting variation. Result: MATCH.
checkpoint_results.motivation: Consignor 'Lantmännen Reppe AB' is explicitly identified in the invoice issuer block at the bottom of the invoice with matching address (Jungmansgatan 8, Lidköping). Country Sweden confirmed via Swedish VAT number prefix SE and IBAN prefix SE. Rule 4.1.0 (corporate structure) gives MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The invoice is from Meelunie B.V. which is a different company than Lantmännen Reppe AB
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The TO/Name field shows Meelunie B.V. as the BUYER, not the invoice issuer/seller. The actual invoice issuer is Lantmännen Reppe AB as shown in the footer block. The document is a Pro Forma Invoice issued BY Lantmännen Reppe AB TO Meelunie B.V.

checkpoint_results.certificate_value: Meelunie America Inc., 3000 Town Center, Suite 2237, Southfield, MI 48075, United States Of America
checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Meelunie America Inc., 3000 Town Center, Suite 2237, Southfield, MI 48075, United States Of America (Recipient adress field)

comparison_steps.description: Identify primary billing field in invoice.
comparison_steps.details: The invoice TO/Name field shows 'Meelunie B.V.' at 'Vinoly Tower - 18Th Floor, Claude Debussylaan 40, 1082 MD Amserdam, NETHERLANDS'. This is a different entity than the certificate consignee 'Meelunie America Inc.'. Certificate consignee does not match the primary billing party.

comparison_steps.step: 4.2.0.3 – Ship-To rule (Certificate issued for end recipient)
comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate consignee matches Ship-To/Recipient address and Ship-To rule conditions are met.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate consignee 'Meelunie America Inc.' is found exactly in the invoice 'Recipient adress' field with identical name, address, and country. The invoice Bill-To party is 'Meelunie B.V.' (Netherlands) — a different entity (parent company / trading entity in a different country). Both parties appear in the same invoice, confirming they are part of the same supply chain. Nothing in the invoice contradicts that Meelunie America Inc. is the actual end recipient of the goods. All conditions of rule 4.2.0.3 are met: (1) Exact match in Ship-To field, (2) Bill-To is a different party, (3) Same invoice contains both, (4) No contradicting information.
Certificate: 'United States Of America'. Invoice Recipient adress: 'United States Of America'. Exact match.

rules_applied.description: Ship-To rule – Certificate of Origin issued for end recipient (Meelunie America Inc.) while invoice is billed to trading entity (Meelunie B.V.). Consignee found exactly in Recipient address field.
Country verification – United States Of America matches exactly

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'Meelunie America Inc., 3000 Town Center, Suite 2237, Southfield, MI 48075, United States Of America'. Invoice primary billing field (TO): 'Meelunie B.V., Vinoly Tower - 18Th Floor, Claude Debussylaan 40, 1082 MD Amserdam, NETHERLANDS'. These are different entities — Meelunie B.V. (Netherlands) vs Meelunie America Inc. (USA). However, the invoice Recipient adress field contains: 'Meelunie America Inc., 3000 Town Center, Suite 2237, Southfield, MI 48075, United States Of America' — an EXACT match with the certificate consignee. Applying rule 4.2.0.3 (Ship-To rule): (1) Certificate consignee matches exactly in Recipient address field ✓, (2) Bill-To party (Meelunie B.V.) is a different entity ✓, (3) Both parties in the same invoice — same supply chain ✓, (4) No contradicting information ✓. This is a standard trade pattern where the COO follows the goods to the end recipient while the invoice follows the payment to the trading company. Result: MATCH.
checkpoint_results.motivation: Certificate consignee 'Meelunie America Inc.' is found exactly in the invoice Recipient address field with identical name, full address, and country. The Ship-To rule (4.2.0.3) applies: the COO is issued for the end recipient while the invoice is billed to the trading entity Meelunie B.V. (Netherlands). All four conditions of the Ship-To rule are met.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because the primary billing party (Meelunie B.V., Netherlands) differs from certificate consignee (Meelunie America Inc., USA)
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.2.0.3 (Ship-To rule) explicitly allows the certificate consignee to be the Ship-To party when the Bill-To party is a different entity (trading company), both appear in the same invoice, and no contradicting information exists. This is a recognized trade pattern in international commerce.

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains article numbers.
comparison_steps.details: No specific article numbers are stated in the certificate goods description. Priority 1 does not apply.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the identity-bearing main designation in the certificate and match against invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate goods description: 'Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB / Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region'. The identity-bearing main designation is 'Organic VWG'. The invoice contains: 'Reppal VWG Organic 25 Kg' in the product line AND the exact text 'Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB / Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region' in the description area. The full text from the certificate is reproduced verbatim in the invoice. The main designation 'Organic VWG' can be identified in the invoice in the exact phrase 'Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB'. The word 'VWG' is the key product identifier and appears in both documents. The invoice also uses 'Reppal VWG Organic' which contains 'VWG' and 'Organic'. Per rule 4.3.4 (non-identity-bearing additions), 'Reppal' is a brand name, '25 Kg' is a packaging specification, and 'Batch No: 2303090003' is an internal reference — none of these change the product identity.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that additional text does not change product identity.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice contains additional text 'Reppal' (brand name), '25 Kg' (packaging size), 'Batch No: 2303090003' (internal reference). These are non-identity-bearing per 4.3.4. The certificate text '20 Pallets' is a packaging/shipping description. The core product description text is identical in both documents.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching – identity-bearing main designation 'Organic VWG' identified in certificate and found verbatim in invoice description text
Non-identity-bearing additions – 'Reppal' (brand), '25 Kg' (packaging), 'Batch No' (internal ref), '20 Pallets' (shipping) do not change product identity

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate box 6 states: '20 Pallets / Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB / Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region'. No article numbers are present, so Priority 2 (text matching) applies. The identity-bearing main designation is 'Organic VWG'. The invoice description contains the EXACT same text: 'Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB / Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region' — a verbatim match. Additionally, the invoice product line 'Reppal VWG Organic 25 Kg' contains 'VWG' and 'Organic', confirming the same product. 'Reppal' is the product brand/trade name and '25 Kg' is a packaging specification — these are non-identity-bearing additions per rule 4.3.4. Result: MATCH.
checkpoint_results.motivation: The certificate's goods description text 'Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB / Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region' appears verbatim in the invoice description area. The identity-bearing main designation 'Organic VWG' is clearly identifiable in both documents. Additional invoice text ('Reppal', '25 Kg', 'Batch No') constitutes non-identity-bearing additions per rule 4.3.4.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because certificate says 'Organic VWG' while invoice says 'Reppal VWG Organic'
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The invoice ALSO contains the exact text 'Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB' in its description section, which is a verbatim match. 'Reppal' is a brand name (non-identity-bearing addition per 4.3.4). The word order variation 'Organic VWG' vs 'VWG Organic' in the product line is moot since the full phrase matches verbatim in the description text.

comparison_steps.description: Check if the numeric value 20000 from the certificate can be identified in the invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate box 7: '20000 Kg'. Invoice quantity column: '20 000,00'. After normalization: removing thousands separator (space) and trailing decimal zeros: 20000,00 → 20000. The numeric value 20000 matches exactly. Per rule 4.4.3, the unit need not match — it is sufficient that the numeric value can be identified. The invoice quantity column shows 20 000,00 which normalizes to 20000.

comparison_steps.description: Normalize thousands separators in invoice quantity.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice shows '20 000,00' — the space is a thousands separator. After removing the space: 20000,00. The ',00' is a decimal part equal to zero. Normalized value: 20000. Certificate value: 20000. Exact match.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that both numeric value and unit are stated in the certificate.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate states '20000 Kg' — numeric value 20000 with explicit unit Kg. Requirement met.

rules_applied.description: Occurrence check – numeric value 20000 identified in invoice quantity column after normalization
Thousands separator normalization – space removed from '20 000,00' to get 20000
Certificate states quantity with explicit unit (20000 Kg)

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7 states '20000 Kg'. The invoice quantity column shows '20 000,00'. After removing the thousands separator (space) per rule 4.4.3.3 and the trailing ',00' (zero decimal): 20000. This exactly matches the certificate value 20000. The certificate explicitly states the unit 'Kg' satisfying rule 4.4.2. Per rule 4.4.3, the invoice need not state the same unit — it is sufficient that the numeric value can be identified. Note: the invoice quantity '20 000,00' appears to be in a generic quantity column (possibly referring to kilograms given the product is sold at 5.20 €/unit with total 104,000 €, and 20000 × 5.20 = 104,000). The numeric value matches. No weight-category issue (GW/NW) applies since the certificate does not specify GW or NW — it simply states '20000 Kg'. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: single weight value without weight category, value can be identified in invoice, and invoice does not contain a conflicting alternative weight value of another category → MATCH.
checkpoint_results.motivation: Certificate states 20000 Kg. Invoice quantity column shows 20 000,00 which normalizes to 20000 after removing thousands separator (space). Exact numeric match. Certificate states explicit unit Kg. No conflicting weight values in invoice.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The invoice quantity '20 000,00' might refer to pieces not kilograms
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.4.3, the invoice need not state the same quantity unit as the certificate. It is sufficient that the numeric value 20000 can be identified in the invoice. The numeric value matches exactly. Additionally, the product description mentions '25 Kg' packaging, suggesting the quantity is weight-related (20000 kg = 800 bags of 25 kg).

checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region (description text)

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin statement in the invoice.
comparison_steps.details: The invoice description text states: 'Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region'. This constitutes an explicit origin statement indicating Sweden as the country of production and EU as the region of raw material origin.

Certificate: 'EU Sweden'. Invoice: 'Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region'. After normalization: Certificate claims origin EU and Sweden. Invoice confirms production in Sweden and wheat from EU Region.
Certificate states 'EU' as part of origin. Invoice text references 'EU Region' for wheat origin. Sweden is an EU member state (EU-27). EU origin verified.

comparison_steps.description: Verify Sweden as EU member state.
comparison_steps.details: Sweden (SE) is a current EU member state. Certificate states 'EU Sweden' and invoice states 'Produced In Sweden'. Sweden is explicitly mentioned in both documents.

comparison_steps.step: 4.5.4.2.1 – EU combined with one specific country
comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'EU Sweden' — EU combined with one specific EU member state.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate origin: 'EU Sweden'. Invoice contains 'Produced In Sweden' (Sweden confirmed) and 'Wheat From EU Region' (EU confirmed). Both components of the origin claim are verified in the invoice text. Per rule 4.5.4.2.1, when certificate states EU combined with one specific EU member state and invoice confirms both EU origin and the specific country, result is MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Origin identification – 'Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region' found in invoice description
EU normalization – 'EU Region' in invoice corresponds to 'EU' in certificate
EU member state – Sweden is EU member, explicitly stated in both documents
EU combined with one specific EU member state – certificate 'EU Sweden', invoice confirms both Sweden and EU

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: 'EU Sweden'. This claims origin as EU and specifically Sweden. The invoice description text states: 'Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB / Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region'. This explicitly confirms: (1) Production in Sweden, and (2) Wheat from EU Region. Sweden is an EU member state (SE in EU-27). Per rule 4.5.4.2.1: certificate states 'EU [one specific EU member state]', invoice contains an explicit origin statement confirming both EU and Sweden — MATCH. No non-EU origins appear in the invoice. No contradicting origin information found.
checkpoint_results.motivation: Certificate origin 'EU Sweden' is verified against the invoice text 'Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region'. Sweden is explicitly confirmed as production country, and EU is confirmed as the wheat origin region. Both components of the certificate's origin claim are present in the invoice. Sweden is an EU member state. Rule 4.5.4.2.1 applies.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The invoice does not have a dedicated 'Country of Origin' field, only description text
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.5.1, origin can be identified via expressions such as 'Made in', 'Goods are of ... origin', or equivalent. 'Produced In Sweden' is an equivalent expression that explicitly states production origin. This is sufficient for identification.

Country verification for consignor via IBAN prefix SE (Sweden)

rule_evaluation_log.details: No LC reference found in certificate. Invoice number 'L101' is not a Letter of Credit reference.
Each file contains exactly one document. Certificate file = certificate. Invoice file = invoice.
Consignor 'Lantmännen Reppe AB' found in invoice issuer block. Consignee 'Meelunie America Inc.' found in Recipient adress field. 2 of 3 criteria met.
Consignor 'Lantmännen Reppe AB' found explicitly in invoice footer/issuer block and in goods description text.
Sweden confirmed via VAT prefix SE and IBAN prefix SE.
Primary billing field (TO) shows Meelunie B.V. (Netherlands), different from certificate consignee. Ship-To rule 4.2.0.3 evaluated.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Ship-To rule – certificate issued for end recipient
rule_evaluation_log.details: All four conditions met: (1) exact match in Recipient address, (2) Bill-To is different party, (3) same invoice, (4) no contradicting information.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Controlled text matching – identity-bearing main designation
rule_evaluation_log.details: Identity-bearing main designation 'Organic VWG' found verbatim in invoice description. Full certificate text reproduced in invoice.
Certificate 20000 Kg. Invoice 20 000,00 → normalized to 20000. Exact numeric match.
Space removed from '20 000' in invoice quantity.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Weight specification – single value without GW/NW category
rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate states single weight value (20000 Kg) without GW/NW category. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: single value, identified in invoice, no conflicting alternative → MATCH.
'EU Region' in invoice corresponds to 'EU' in certificate. Sweden is EU member state.
Sweden is current EU member. Explicitly stated in both documents.

rule_evaluation_log.description: EU combined with one specific EU member state
rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate 'EU Sweden', invoice confirms both Sweden (production) and EU (wheat region). Both components verified.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter har verifierats framgångsrikt. Avsändaren Lantmännen Reppe AB finns i fakturans utfärdarblock, mottagaren Meelunie America Inc. finns i fakturans mottagaradress, varubeskrivningen 'Organic VWG' återfinns ordagrant i fakturan, kvantiteten 20 000 kg stämmer överens, och ursprungslandet EU/Sverige bekräftas i fakturatexten. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: All five checkpoints verified as MATCH. Consignor identified via corporate structure rule (4.1.0) in invoice issuer block. Consignee identified via Ship-To rule (4.2.0.3) in Recipient address field. Goods description verbatim match. Quantity 20000 exact numeric match after thousands separator normalization. Origin EU Sweden confirmed by invoice production statement.

sections.rule_applied: Koncernstruktur (4.1.0) – consignor-namn hittades i fakturans utfärdarblock. Land verifierat via VAT-prefix SE (4.1.2.1).
sections.motivation: Consignor 'Lantmännen Reppe AB' återfinns explicit i fakturans utfärdarblock längst ned med matchande adress (Jungmansgatan 8, Lidköping). Landet Sverige bekräftas via VAT-nummer med prefix SE och IBAN med prefix SE.

sections.rule_applied: Ship-To-regeln (4.2.0.3) – certifikatet utfärdat för slutmottagaren. Consignee återfinns i fakturans mottagaradressfält. Fakturans Bill-To-part (Meelunie B.V., Nederländerna) är ett handelsbolag.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets consignee 'Meelunie America Inc.' med fullständig adress och land återfinns exakt i fakturans 'Recipient adress'-fält. Ship-To-regeln tillämpas då fakturans Bill-To-part är en annan juridisk person (Meelunie B.V., Nederländerna), båda parter förekommer i samma faktura, och inget i fakturan motsäger att Meelunie America Inc. är slutmottagaren.

sections.motivation: Certifikatets varubeskrivning 'Organic VWG From Lantmännen Reppe AB / Produced In Sweden With Wheat From EU Region' återfinns ordagrant i fakturans beskrivningstext. Ytterligare text i fakturan ('Reppal', '25 Kg', 'Batch No') utgör icke-identitetsbärande tillägg.

sections.rule_applied: Förekomstkontroll (4.4.1) med tusentalsavskiljarnormalisering (4.4.3.3). Enkel viktvärde utan GW/NW-kategori (4.4.2.2 punkt 2).
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 20000 Kg. Fakturans kvantitetskolumn visar '20 000,00' som efter normalisering (borttagning av tusentalsavskiljare) ger 20000. Exakt numerisk matchning. Inga motstridiga viktvärden i fakturan.

output_quality_checks.notes: All five checkpoints evaluated. All statuses MATCH. Minimum confidence across checkpoints is 0.93 (above 0.85 threshold). comparison_result IDENTICAL is consistent with all-MATCH checkpoints. No MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW found.
